title: papaver fugax var. microcarpum
intro: Papaver fugax var. microcarpum, also known as the small-fruited poppy or dwarf poppy, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the Papaver genus. This plant is known for its potential medicinal properties, including its use in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Papaver fugax var. microcarpum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support respiratory health and promote overall well-being.
